hypothesis: The interpolation weight κ in Score-Based Interpolation implicitly encodes a one-dimensional projection of the reaction coordinate's progress, and therefore systematically varying κ across the range [0,1] during inference—rather than fixing it at the equal-density isosurface—will generate a sequence of structurally distinct intermediate configurations that collectively approximate the minimum energy path between metastable states, enabling ASTRA to reconstruct full reaction pathways with committor values monotonically spanning [0,1] without requiring any additional path-finding optimization or transition path sampling.
